name: LogRocket API
what_it_does: Provides session replay, error tracking, and product analytics for web applications via browser SDK instrumentation, with a limited REST API and webhook integration for querying session data and triggering external workflows on session events.
use_cases: Consume LogRocket webhooks to trigger agent workflows when a user session encounters a critical error or rage click event
Query the REST API to retrieve session metadata and error summaries for feeding into automated bug triage pipelines
Correlate LogRocket session IDs with backend logs by injecting the session URL into server-side tracing spans via the JavaScript SDK
Automatically create issue tracker tickets by consuming session error webhooks and enriching them with session replay URLs
Monitor product health by polling session error rate metrics from the API to trigger alerting or incident response agents
not_for: Server-side or backend-only observability — LogRocket's core value is browser session replay, not infrastructure or API monitoring
High-frequency programmatic data extraction at scale — the REST API is limited in scope and not designed for bulk data warehouse export
Real-time structured log aggregation pipelines — tools like Datadog or Grafana Loki are more appropriate for log management
best_when: Best when an agent needs to react to frontend user session events (errors, rages clicks, conversions) via webhooks to drive automated triage or customer success workflows.
avoid_when: Avoid when the primary need is backend API observability or structured log querying, as LogRocket's API surface area is narrow compared to its SDK-based instrumentation capabilities.

auth.notes: REST API uses an API key passed in the Authorization header. Webhook payloads include a signature for verification. SDK initialization uses a public application ID that is safe to embed client-side.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free tier available with limited sessions per month and reduced data retention; specific limits vary by current plan offerings
pricing.paid_tiers: Team plan from ~$99/month; Business and Enterprise plans with higher session volumes and data retention; custom enterprise pricing available
pricing.notes: Pricing is session-volume based. The REST API and webhook features are available on paid plans. Free tier is suitable for evaluation and small-scale use.

performance.rate_limits: REST API rate limits are not prominently documented; the API is not designed for high-frequency polling and should be used sparingly

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API error responses are basic and not comprehensively documented. Webhook payloads are better documented than the REST endpoints.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency support in the REST API. Webhook events may be delivered more than once; handlers must be idempotent using event IDs.
known_agent_gotchas: The REST API is intentionally limited — LogRocket is primarily an SDK-instrumented product, and agents expecting a rich programmatic interface will be disappointed
Session data availability is delayed by several minutes after a session ends; polling immediately after events yields empty results
Webhook payloads contain session URLs but not full session data — agents must direct humans to LogRocket UI rather than extracting replay data programmatically
The API does not support bulk session export, making it unsuitable for building data warehouse pipelines or large-scale offline analysis
API documentation is sparse and occasionally out of date relative to the actual API behavior, requiring trial-and-error for some endpoints

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 Type II certified. HIPAA BAA available on enterprise plans. Webhook signatures should be verified to prevent spoofed event injection. Session data captured by LogRocket may include sensitive user input — PII scrubbing configuration is critical for compliance.
